issues.title: No drawing version control or revision comparison
issues.description: src/api/endpoints/documents.ts getDrawings returns a flat list with no revision history, supersession tracking, or version diffing. Construction drawings go through dozens of revisions; architects issue ASIs and addenda. Without baseline vs. current comparison, field crews work from wrong drawings.
issues.prompt: In src/api/endpoints/documents.ts, extend getDrawings to fetch drawing revisions: add a second Supabase query to `drawing_revisions` table (columns: id, drawing_id, revision_number, issued_date, issued_by, change_description, file_url, superseded_at). Merge results so each drawing has a `revisions: DrawingRevision[]` array sorted descending by revision_number and a `currentRevision` field pointing to the latest non-superseded entry. Add a `getDrawingRevisionHistory(drawingId: string)` export that returns the full revision chain. In src/components/drawings/VersionCompare.tsx, implement a side-by-side PDF overlay that highlights changed regions using canvas diff (pixel delta > threshold shows red overlay). Add a `RevisionBadge` component that renders 'Rev 3' in amber if revision > 0, red if issued in last 48h. Acceptance criteria: (1) drawing list shows current revision number and issued date; (2) clicking a drawing opens VersionCompare showing prev vs current with change highlights; (3) superseded drawings are visually flagged and non-clickable for field use; (4) revision history panel shows full chain with change descriptions.

issues.title: No RLS enforcement on file and drawing queries
issues.description: src/api/endpoints/documents.ts getDrawings and getFiles query Supabase tables with only project_id filtering applied client-side via validateProjectId. There is no server-side RLS policy verification. A user with a valid project_id from another organization can read drawings and files if they manipulate the client. This is a critical multi-tenant data exposure.
issues.prompt: In src/api/middleware/projectScope.ts, add an `assertProjectBelongsToOrg(projectId: string, orgId: string)` async function that queries `projects` table with both project_id and organization_id checks before allowing any document fetch. In src/api/endpoints/documents.ts, import this function and call it at the top of both getDrawings and getFiles, passing the orgId from the active organization context (src/stores/organizationStore.ts). Add a Supabase RLS policy migration (document in src/lib/rls.ts) that enforces: `auth.uid() IN (SELECT user_id FROM project_members WHERE project_id = drawings.project_id)` for both `drawings` and `files` tables. Add an integration test in src/test/integration/lifecycles.test.ts covering cross-org document access rejection. Acceptance criteria: (1) requests without org membership return 403; (2) RLS policy blocks direct Supabase client calls bypassing API layer; (3) existing authorized tests pass.

issues.title: getFiles returns itemCount: 0 hardcoded for all folders
issues.description: In src/api/endpoints/documents.ts, the getFiles mapper sets `itemCount: 0` as a hardcode for every entry. Folder item counts are never calculated. PMs and supers cannot see at a glance how many documents are in each folder, making the file tree navigation useless for large projects with 5,000+ documents.
issues.prompt: In src/api/endpoints/documents.ts, after fetching all files for a project, compute folder item counts in a post-processing step: group all files by parent_folder_id, then for each folder entry set `itemCount` to the count of direct children. Use a Map<string, number> keyed by folder id built in O(n). Also add a `totalSize` field per folder summing file_size of all descendants (recursive via a helper function). Update the FileRow type in src/types/api.ts to include `parent_folder_id: string | null`. In src/pages/Files.tsx (or equivalent file browser component), render the itemCount as '14 items' in the table row and totalSize as '234 MB' for folder rows. Add a `lastModified` field showing the most recent child file's created_at. Acceptance criteria: (1) folder rows show correct item count matching actual child count; (2) item count updates when files are added/removed without page reload; (3) empty folders show '0 items' with a distinct empty state; (4) no hardcoded zeros in production data.

issues.title: No bulk file operations, drag-and-drop upload, or keyboard navigation
issues.description: src/components/files/UploadZone.tsx exists but there is no bulk selection with shift-click, no drag-and-drop folder reorganization, no keyboard navigation (J/K up/down), and no bulk actions bar (move, delete, download ZIP). This is worse than Google Drive and far below the Linear/Figma standard the product aspires to.
issues.prompt: In src/components/files/UploadZone.tsx, implement HTML5 drag-and-drop for both files and folder trees (use webkitdirectory attribute). In src/components/shared/DataTable.tsx, ensure the checkbox column supports shift-click range selection. Create src/components/shared/BulkActionBar.tsx (file may already exist—extend it) to include document-specific actions: Download as ZIP (use JSZip), Move to Folder (folder picker modal), Delete Selected (with confirmation), Share Link. In src/pages/Files.tsx, add useTableKeyboardNavigation hook (src/hooks/useTableKeyboardNavigation.ts) so J/K move selection, Enter opens file preview, Space toggles selection. Add a breadcrumb trail (src/components/Breadcrumbs.tsx) for folder depth navigation. Acceptance criteria: (1) shift-click selects range of files; (2) bulk action bar appears with count when items selected; (3) J/K keyboard navigation works in file table; (4) drag-and-drop from desktop uploads and shows progress; (5) folder drag-and-drop reorganizes hierarchy.

issues.title: Zero AI integration in document management — no auto-tagging, no search, no clash detection
issues.description: The entire documents module (src/api/endpoints/documents.ts, src/pages/Files.tsx, src/components/drawings/) has no AI features. No auto-captioning on upload, no full-text semantic search, no AI-driven drawing issue flagging. Competitors like Autodesk Construction Cloud offer AI clash detection. This is the single biggest competitive gap.
issues.prompt: In src/lib/aiService.ts, add `analyzeDrawingSheet(drawingId: string, fileUrl: string): Promise<DrawingAnalysis>` that calls the AI provider with a vision prompt identifying: sheet type (architectural/structural/MEP), drawing number, revision, potential coordination conflicts (overlapping elements between disciplines). Add `tagDocumentOnUpload(fileId: string, fileName: string, fileUrl: string): Promise<string[]>` that returns suggested tags. In src/components/files/UploadZone.tsx, call tagDocumentOnUpload after successful upload and pre-populate tag field. In src/components/files/DocumentSearch.tsx (extend existing file), implement semantic search using embeddings stored in a `document_embeddings` Supabase table—query via cosine similarity. Add an AI Insights panel to the drawings page showing: 'Drawing A-201 Rev 3 conflicts with S-101 at Grid Line C4' with confidence score. Add inline AI button on each drawing row: 'Analyze Sheet' triggers analyzeDrawingSheet. Acceptance criteria: (1) uploaded files get AI-suggested tags; (2) semantic search returns relevant results beyond keyword match; (3) drawing analysis identifies sheet metadata; (4) coordination conflicts surfaced with location reference.

issues.title: disciplineColor returns raw string not a design-token color — breaks visual discipline coding
issues.description: In src/api/endpoints/documents.ts, getDrawings maps `disciplineColor: d.discipline || 'gray'`. This passes raw discipline strings like 'Architectural' or 'Mechanical' as color values directly to the UI, which then has no color mapping. Drawing sets need consistent color-coding by discipline (Arch=blue, Structural=red, MEP=green, Civil=orange) matching industry standards.
issues.prompt: In src/api/endpoints/documents.ts, replace the `disciplineColor` mapping with a `getDisciplineColor(discipline: string): string` pure function that maps: { 'Architectural': '#3B82F6', 'Structural': '#E74C3C', 'Mechanical': '#F47820', 'Electrical': '#F5A623', 'Plumbing': '#4EC896', 'Civil': '#8B5CF6', 'Landscape': '#10B981' } with a fallback of '#6B7280'. Also add `disciplineLabel: string` and `disciplineIcon: string` (Lucide icon name) to the mapped drawing object. In src/pages/Drawings.tsx (or the drawings list component), render a colored dot badge using the disciplineColor alongside the sheet title. Add filter tabs above the drawing list for each discipline. Update src/types/api.ts DrawingRow to include a `discipline` string field. Acceptance criteria: (1) each discipline renders with its correct color badge; (2) filter tabs correctly filter the list; (3) no raw discipline strings used as CSS color values anywhere.

issues.title: No empty states, no loading skeletons, no error boundaries in documents/drawings pages
issues.description: src/components/files/FilePreview.tsx and the drawings components have no skeleton loaders, no empty state components, and no error boundaries. On a slow jobsite connection a blank white page renders for several seconds. Empty projects show nothing. Per design standards, every empty state must have illustration, message, and a primary CTA.
issues.prompt: Create src/components/files/DocumentsEmptyState.tsx: renders a folder icon (Lucide FolderOpen, 48px, muted color), heading 'No documents yet', subtext 'Upload drawings, specs, contracts, and reports to keep your project organized', and a primary 'Upload Files' button. Create src/components/drawings/DrawingsEmptyState.tsx similarly with LayersIcon. In src/pages/Files.tsx and src/pages/Drawings.tsx, wrap data-fetching sections in React Suspense with skeleton loaders (gray rectangles at 48px row height, pulsing) matching the table shape. Add an ErrorBoundary (src/components/ErrorBoundary.tsx already exists—use it) around each page's main content area with a 'Failed to load documents. Retry' state. In src/components/files/FilePreview.tsx, add a loading skeleton matching the preview pane shape while the file URL resolves. Acceptance criteria: (1) skeleton rows render immediately on page load; (2) empty state shows with upload CTA on fresh projects; (3) network failure shows retry state not blank screen; (4) previews show shimmer while loading.

issues.title: No real-time collaboration on drawings — no presence indicators or live markup sync
issues.description: src/components/drawings/DrawingViewer.tsx and MarkupToolbar.tsx have no real-time presence or markup synchronization. If two users mark up the same drawing simultaneously their edits conflict silently. Procore and Fieldwire both support real-time drawing collaboration. This is a critical gap for field coordination.
issues.prompt: In src/components/drawings/DrawingViewer.tsx, integrate the Liveblocks presence system (src/lib/liveblocks.ts already exists). On mount, join a room keyed as `drawing:${drawingId}` and subscribe to others' cursor positions and active markups. Render remote cursors as colored dots with user initials (reuse src/components/shared/PresenceAvatars.tsx). In src/components/drawings/MarkupToolbar.tsx, broadcast each markup action (add annotation, move, delete) as a Liveblocks event and apply remote events to local canvas state. Store committed markups to Supabase `drawing_markups` table (columns: id, drawing_id, revision_id, markup_type, coordinates, color, text, created_by, created_at). Add a presence bar (src/components/collaboration/PresenceBar.tsx) above the drawing viewer showing who is currently viewing. Acceptance criteria: (1) two users opening same drawing see each other's cursors; (2) markup added by User A appears within 500ms for User B; (3) presence bar shows active viewers; (4) markups persist to Supabase on save.

issues.title: DrawingViewer canvas has no keyboard accessibility or ARIA labels
issues.description: src/components/drawings/DrawingViewer.tsx and PdfViewer.tsx render to canvas elements with no ARIA roles, no keyboard zoom/pan controls, and no focus management. Screen readers announce nothing. WCAG 2.1 AA requires that all interactive elements are keyboard-operable and screen-reader-accessible.
issues.prompt: In src/components/drawings/DrawingViewer.tsx, add `role='application'` and `aria-label='Drawing viewer - use arrow keys to pan, plus/minus to zoom'` to the canvas container div. Implement keyboard handlers: ArrowKeys pan the viewport by 50px, +/- zoom by 10%, Home resets to fit. Add `tabIndex={0}` to the container so it receives focus. In src/components/drawings/MarkupToolbar.tsx, ensure each tool button has `aria-label` (e.g. 'Cloud markup tool'), `aria-pressed` state when active, and is keyboard-operable. Add a visually-hidden live region (src/components/ui/LiveRegion.tsx) that announces: 'Zoomed to 150%', 'Annotation added at grid C4'. In src/components/drawings/PdfViewer.tsx, add page navigation controls with `aria-label='Page X of Y'` and keyboard shortcuts (PageUp/PageDown). Acceptance criteria: (1) axe-core reports zero violations on drawing pages; (2) all toolbar buttons keyboard-operable; (3) zoom/pan keyboard controls functional; (4) screen reader announces state changes via live region.

issues.title: DrawingRow and FileRow types use implicit any casts with spread operator
issues.description: In src/api/endpoints/documents.ts both getDrawings and getFiles use `...d` and `...f` spread on raw Supabase rows before the types are narrowed. The additional mapped fields are appended without type guards, meaning TypeScript infers the return type as an intersection that silently accepts unknown shapes. Several fields like `content_type` are accessed without null checks.
issues.prompt: In src/types/api.ts, define strict interfaces for DrawingRow and FileRow with all Supabase columns explicitly typed (no implicit any). Create a `MappedDrawing` interface extending DrawingRow with the additional computed fields: `setNumber: string`, `disciplineColor: string`, `disciplineLabel: string`, `date: string`, `sheetCount: number`, `revisions: DrawingRevision[]`. Create a `MappedFile` interface with: `type: 'folder' | 'file'`, `size: string`, `modifiedDate: string`, `itemCount: number`. In src/api/endpoints/documents.ts, replace spread-and-append patterns with explicit object construction satisfying `MappedDrawing` and `MappedFile`. Add null-safe access for `content_type`: `const isFolder = f.content_type?.includes('folder') ?? false`. Enable `strict: true` in tsconfig if not already set. Add return type annotations to both exported functions. Acceptance criteria: (1) no TypeScript errors with strict mode; (2) return types are explicit named interfaces; (3) no `as any` or implicit any in documents.ts; (4) all field accesses are null-safe.

invented_features.title: AI Drawing Delta Engine
invented_features.description: When a new drawing revision is issued, automatically compare it to the previous revision using computer vision, extract a list of changes in plain English ('Column C4 moved 6 inches east', 'Door 201A removed'), and automatically flag all RFIs, submittals, and change orders that reference affected areas. Push notifications to relevant subs with affected areas highlighted. This makes drawing revision management proactive instead of reactive and is something no competitor offers today.

invented_features.title: Smart Spec-to-Submittal Auto-Generator
invented_features.description: On project setup, parse the uploaded specification book (PDF) using AI to automatically generate the entire submittal register with spec section numbers, required submittal types (shop drawings, product data, samples), required approval dates back-calculated from the schedule, and responsible subcontractor pre-filled from the buyout log. What takes a PM 8 hours today becomes a 30-second AI action.

invented_features.title: Field Drawing Pin — One-Tap Issue Linking
invented_features.description: On mobile, a super taps a location on a drawing thumbnail, which opens a context menu: 'Create RFI here', 'Log Punch Item here', 'Capture Photo here'. The GPS coordinates and drawing sheet/location are auto-attached. All issues become spatially indexed on the drawing set, giving the PM a heat map of problem areas on the project. This rivals Fieldwire's plan-linked tasks but with deeper RFI/CO integration.

competitive_intel: Procore's document module requires training to navigate and lacks real-time collaboration on drawings. Autodesk Construction Cloud has superior BIM integration but its 2D drawing workflow is fragmented from PlanGrid. Fieldwire has excellent mobile drawing markup but no version control intelligence and no financial document linking. SiteSyncAI's document module as audited is below all three competitors on version control, AI integration, and real-time collaboration. The VersionCompare component exists as a stub but is not wired to revision data. The drawings endpoint is the weakest API in the codebase. To differentiate, SiteSync must lead on: (1) AI-powered revision delta detection no competitor has, (2) drawings spatially linked to every RFI/submittal/punch item, and (3) real-time multi-user markup that works offline with conflict resolution on sync.
summary: The Document Management module is the lowest-scoring module audited to date, sitting at 40/100. The API layer (src/api/endpoints/documents.ts) is critically thin: drawings return no revision history, files return hardcoded zeros for folder counts, and neither table has enforced RLS policies creating a multi-tenant security exposure. There is zero AI integration in the entire module — no auto-tagging on upload, no semantic search, no drawing analysis — making it categorically behind Autodesk Construction Cloud. The VersionCompare component exists as a file stub but is not connected to revision data. No real-time collaboration exists on drawings despite Liveblocks being available in the codebase. Empty states are generic or absent, loading skeletons are missing, and the canvas-based drawing viewer has no keyboard accessibility. The disciplineColor field passes raw strings as CSS colors, breaking the visual discipline-coding system entirely. To reach competitive parity with Procore and leapfrog to category leadership, the module needs: enforced RLS, full revision control with AI delta detection, real-time drawing collaboration via Liveblocks, semantic document search, and spatially-indexed issue linking from drawings. These are 6-8 weeks of focused engineering effort.
